meaning: adj.有教训意味的,教训性的,有益的
examples: It all brought back memories of a not very edifying past.
In the 18th century art was seen, along with music and poetry, as something edifying.
Not that the edifying aspect of the story can he considered probable.
Veterans benefits have been around for ages, and their track record is none too edifying.
Also, perhaps in consequence of its relative globality, its presentations are particularly well done and edifying.
